Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) on base appMoveOpsOps001 for Vectral Systems stipend records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records. Finds 5 false rejections from the pilot audit (Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet setup, Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh WiFi). Kevin Tran's fraud-flagged $89 keyboard receipt (STIP-2026-0089) was already paid out 2026-03-08 with no admin hold. Post-audit $45 monitor stand submission (recStip000000034, Approved, TechComfort Supply template match).
2. Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) on base appMoveOpsOps001 for Mosaic Robotics stipend records. Finds 3 false approvals from the pilot audit (Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk exceeding $800 limit, Andre Washington $350 coworking membership excluded category, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking day passes excluded category). Andre Washington's fraud-flagged $89 keyboard receipt (STIP-2026-0094, admin hold, identical SHA-256 hash to Kevin Tran's).
3. Search emails using search_emails with query related to "audit" or "duplicate" or "stipend." Finds Dmitri's email to Marcus (dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com) detailing duplicate receipt anomalies across 3 clients with SHA-256 hash matches and $223 total exposure. Also Samira's audit summary to Elena (47 expenses, 83% accuracy, 5 false rejections Vectral, 3 false approvals Mosaic, $885 exposure). Marcus's reply emails with QuickBooks status per STIP transaction.
4. Search Linear using linear_list_issues with query related to "audit" or "expense" or "duplicate." Finds ENG-216 (full audit of 47 expenses, assigned to Dmitri) with the list of affected records, and ENG-220 (duplicate receipt detection) with SHA-256 hash findings linking Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, and Jordan Ekwueme to TechComfort Supply.
5. Search Linear comments using linear_list_comments on ENG-216 or ENG-220. Finds Dmitri's detailed audit breakdown listing each false rejection and false approval with employee names, amounts, and reasons. Duplicate receipt hash matches and PDF metadata template patterns (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041).
6. Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills with query related to "stipend" or "hold" or "STIP." Finds STIP-2026-0089 ($89, Kevin Tran, Vectral) — PAID 2026-03-08, balance $0, no admin hold. STIP-2026-0094 ($89, Andre Washington, Mosaic) — ADMIN HOLD placed 2026-03-15 by Marcus Thorne. STIP-2026-0097 ($45, Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop) — ADMIN HOLD placed 2026-03-15 by Marcus Thorne. Total on hold: $134.
7. Search Slack #finance channel (C005) using conversations_search_messages with query related to "stipend" or "hold." Finds Marcus announcing holds on STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097 ($134 total on hold, $223 total potential loss). Hana tracking STIP-0089, 0094, 0097 with TechComfort ledger cross-check. Elena's directive mandating $100 manual verification threshold.
8. Look up Marcus Thorne's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Marcus." Email: marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
9. Send email via send_email from hana.kim@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com. Categorized list: records cleared for payment, records still held (STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097 with active admin holds; STIP-2026-0089 already paid, clawback pending HR verification), records needing further investigation (recStip000000034 TechComfort template match, uncorrected false approvals).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

4 The email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (visible in send_email parameters) identifies STIP-2026-0089 ($89, Kevin Tran) as already paid out before the fraud was discovered, noting the clawback consideration.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email explicitly identifies Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard as 'already paid out on 2026-03-08' and notes 'If the receipt is determined to be fraudulent, we may need to pursue recovery of the $89 already paid.' The criterion allows for 'clawback' or 'already paid' references, both are present.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states: 'Note: Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard (STIP-2026-0089) was already paid on 2026-03-08 before the duplicate was discovered. That disbursement cannot be reversed...' This correctly identifies the already-paid status and implicitly notes the clawback consideration.
✓Run #3The email explicitly mentions 'Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard (STIP-2026-0089) was already paid out on 3/8 before the audit' and notes 'No recovery action needed unless further fraud investigation warrants it,' which captures the clawback consideration.
✓Run #4The email explicitly notes: 'Note: STIP-2026-0089 (Kevin Tran, Vectral, $89 keyboard) was already paid out on 3/8. Rachel Whitfield at Vectral has since confirmed Kevin's purchase was legitimate in her renewal correspondence. No recovery action needed on that one, but the duplicate hash with Andre Washington's submission remains unexplained.' This identifies the already-paid status and considers clawback/recovery.
✓Run #5The email body includes 'STIP-2026-0089' in Section 3, describes Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard as 'PAID on 2026-03-08, Balance $0', and explicitly recommends 'we may need to pursue recovery of the $89' — addressing both the already-paid status and the clawback consideration.
✓Run #6The email explicitly states for STIP-2026-0089: 'Already paid out 2026-03-08 (Balance $0). This is the matching duplicate — same byte-for-byte PDF. Cannot claw back without further action.' Kevin Tran $89 is correctly identified as already paid and clawback is mentioned.
5 The email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (visible in send_email parameters) flags the post-audit Kevin Tran $45 monitor stand submission (recStip000000034) as needing investigation due to TechComfort Supply template match.
outcome
 ✗✓✓✓✗✗ 50%
✗Run #1The email lists Kevin Tran's $45 monitor stand (April 5) under 'RECORDS CLEARED FOR PAYMENT' as item #7, not flagged for investigation. There is no mention of TechComfort Supply template match for this record or recStip000000034 in either the email or the final response.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly flags 'Kevin Tran — Monitor Stand — $45 (recStip000000034, dated 2026-04-05)' under Section 3 (NEEDS FURTHER INVESTIGATION), noting that 'The receipt is from TechComfort Supply with PDF /Creator metadata matching the same invoice generator used in the flagged duplicate cluster.'
✓Run #3The email explicitly flags recStip000000034 in the 'NEEDS FURTHER INVESTIGATION' section, identifying Kevin Tran's $45 monitor stand (April 5) with 'Receipt from TechComfort Supply with PDF Creator metadata matching the flagged template cluster.'
✓Run #4The email flags this in a 'REQUIRES FURTHER INVESTIGATION' section, explicitly listing recStip000000034 for Kevin Tran's $45 monitor stand with the TechComfort Supply vendor match and receipt hash matching Kenji Nakamura's coworking pass receipt.
✗Run #5The email explicitly clears Kevin Tran's $45 monitor stand for payment (Section 1, item 7) with the note 'Post-audit submission. Different vendor/receipt from TechComfort duplicate cluster. Clean.' The agent did not flag this as needing investigation, did not reference recStip000000034, and did not identify a TechComfort Supply template match. This is the opposite of what the criterion requires.
✗Run #6The email lists recStip000000034 (Kevin Tran $45 monitor stand) under 'Cleared for Payment' as 'Approved. Within policy. No issues.' The agent did not flag this post-audit submission as needing investigation due to TechComfort Supply template pattern matching.
